name: Ramp API
what_it_does: Ramp's REST API enables programmatic control of corporate cards, spend limits, expense approvals, reimbursements, and accounting sync — purpose-built for finance automation and spend management at scale.
use_cases: Automatically issuing virtual cards with custom spend limits for agent-driven procurement
Syncing Ramp transactions and categorizations to accounting systems (QuickBooks, NetSuite)
Monitoring and enforcing spend policies from automated finance workflows
Processing expense reimbursements and approvals without manual review
Building real-time spend dashboards with Ramp transaction webhook data
not_for: Consumer expense tracking (Ramp is B2B corporate cards only)
International cards in all geographies (Ramp is US-centric)
Companies needing physical card programs before virtual card approval
best_when: Your company uses Ramp for corporate spend and you need agents to issue cards, set limits, or sync expenses to your accounting stack automatically.
avoid_when: You need global card issuance or are outside the US — Brex or Airwallex may have better international coverage.

auth.notes: OAuth 2.0 client credentials for server-to-server. Scopes map to specific resource operations (transactions:read, cards:write, users:read). Access tokens expire in 1 hour. Requires Ramp Business account with developer access enabled.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Ramp is free for businesses — revenue model is interchange fees, not SaaS
pricing.paid_tiers: Ramp Plus for enhanced controls; Enterprise for custom. No API fees.
pricing.notes: Ramp's platform is free because they earn interchange on card spend. API access is included. Apply for a Ramp business account to get API credentials.

performance.rate_limits: Not publicly documented; reasonable limits apply

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Standard REST errors with error_code and message fields. Clear scope error messages when permissions missing. Rate limit responses include appropriate headers. OpenAPI spec available for code generation.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Idempotency keys supported on card creation and transaction endpoints. Check Ramp docs for specific endpoint support.
known_agent_gotchas: OAuth scopes are narrow — agents must request specific scopes at authorization time; cannot add scopes without re-auth
Virtual card creation requires a spending entity — agents must know the department/entity ID before issuing cards
Access tokens expire in 1 hour — agents in long-running workflows must refresh proactively
Ramp is US-only — attempting to create cards for non-US entities fails silently or returns confusing errors
Webhook events for spend require signature validation — missed validation is a fraud risk in financial workflows

security_score_components.security_notes: OAuth 2.0 with fine-grained scopes. PCI-DSS compliant. SOC 2 Type II. Card data never returned in full — PAN masked. Webhook HMAC signatures for payload validation.
